---
title: "Sentiment <br>Analysis"
subtitle: Day 15
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: ../img/horst_stringr.jpeg
  data-background-size: 70%
  data-background-position: bottom right
  data-background-opacity: "0.8"
  data-slide-number: none
format: 
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
    code-copy: true
    code-link: true
    scrollable: true
auto-stretch: false
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

library(countdown)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(patchwork)
library(ggthemes)
library(nycflights23)
library(here)
slides_theme = theme_minimal(
  base_family = "Atkinson Hyperlegible",
  base_size = 16)

theme_set(slides_theme)

set.seed(0206)
```

## Today

1. Sentiment analysis
2. Lab Quiz 2! 

## {background-image="../img/coursera-data-sci.png"}

## Data for today

Random (?) sample of 26,882 reviews of coursera courses

```{r}
en_coursera_reviews <- read_csv("https://stat220-w26.github.io/data/en_coursera_sample.csv")
en_coursera_reviews
```

::: aside
Source: [Kaggle](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/septa97/100k-courseras-course-reviews-dataset/data)

:::



## Are these positive or negative reviews?

"thank you so much it was great course"  

"Too reliant on materials directly collected by professor, too little context of larger artistic community." 

"Excellent! easy to follow and engaging!"  

"Slow and redundant. Would have preferred a faster-paced and more substantive course." 

"Way too shallow and way too english :("   

## Sentiment analysis

One way to analyze the *sentiment* is to consider the text as a combination of the individual words. It's computationally convenient because we're able to use the `tidy` tools that we've been building up: 

1. *tokenize* the text
2. *join* the sentiment values to each token
3. *group* the words in the document to *summarize* the overall sentiment

## Sentiment datasets: `bing`

```{r}
#| output-location: column
library(tidytext)
get_sentiments("bing") %>%
  slice_sample(n = 20)
```

## Sentiment datasets: `afinn`

```{r}
#| output-location: column
library(textdata)
get_sentiments("afinn") %>%
  slice_sample(n = 20)
```

## 

```{r}
en_coursera_reviews 
```

## `inner_join`

![](../img/inner-join.gif)

## `join`ing the sentiment data

```{r}
#| output-location: column-fragment
#| code-line-numbers: "1|2-3|4"
bing_sentiments = get_sentiments("bing")
en_coursera_reviews %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, Review) %>% 
  inner_join(bing_sentiments, by = "word") %>%
  select(-c(CourseId, cld2))
```

## `group_by` review ID, `summarize` overall sentiment

```{r}
#| output-location: column-fragment
#| code-line-numbers: "4|5-8"
bing_review_scores <- en_coursera_reviews %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, Review) %>% 
  inner_join(bing_sentiments, by = "word") %>%
  group_by(review_id) %>%
  summarize(
    sum = (sum(sentiment == "positive") - sum(sentiment == "negative"))
  )

bing_review_scores
```

## Most positive review (by `sum`): 

```{r}
#| output-location: column-fragment
#| code-line-numbers: "1|2|3|4"

en_coursera_reviews %>%
  left_join(bing_review_scores, by = "review_id") %>%
  slice_max(sum) %>%
  pull(Review)
```

## Most negative reviews (by `sum`): 

```{r}
#| output-location: column

en_coursera_reviews %>%
  left_join(bing_review_scores, by = "review_id") %>%
  slice_min(sum) %>%
  pull(Review) %>%
  pluck(1)
```

# What weaknesses do you notice about this analysis? 

What could we change?

# Live coding time
